destinations.evidence: The port defines a DKMS-specific fixed executor fee constant.
DKMS fee calculation always uses the fixed fee and a 20-byte output for inclusion fee input.
Wallet validation uses the complete fixed DKMS total as the escrow requirement.

equivalence_argument: The fee dispatcher, fee helper, and wallet validation all converge on calculate_dkms_fees(input_len), whose executor fee is fixed and whose inclusion output size is the constant 20-byte DKMS output.
notes: This maps directly to the current fixed-fee DKMS implementation.
rationale: The diff adds a DKMS-specific fee constant and fee functions, routes calculate_async_fees to calculate_dkms_fees for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE, and updates wallet escrow to call dkms_escrow_lockup instead of deriving an HTTP-style fee. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For the DKMS key precompile at 0x081B, async fee calculation must use the fixed DKMS_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI executor fee, assume a 20-byte DKMS output for validator fee inputs, and make dkms_escrow_lockup(input_len) equal the complete DKMS fee total.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call calculate_async_fees for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE with different input/output lengths and assert executor_fee always equals DKMS_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI, total equals calculate_dkms_fees(input_len).total, and dkms_escrow_lockup(input_len) matches that total.
summary: DKMS uses a fixed executor fee and 20-byte output fee inputs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call calculate_async_fees for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE with different input/output lengths and assert executor_fee always equals DKMS_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI, total equals calculate_dkms_fees(input_len).total, and dkms_escrow_lockup(input_len) matches that total.
title: DKMS uses fixed executor fee
